manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: pipe, file, bow, mouth, watch, force, shoe, lock, embassy.
Your opponent's team words are: mass, jam, switch, pole, hood, spring, limousine, engine.
Distractor words are: duck, server, london.
The kill words are: train, calf, bank, bar, soul.

message.content:
CLUE: security
TARGETS: lock, embassy, watch, force
message.reasoning:
The user asks: Provide a single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words. Must follow format CLUE: <WORD> TARGETS: <TARGETS>. No extra text.

We must ensure clue is not any of the listed words. Also cannot contain parts of them. Must be semantically related. Choose which team words to target.

Our team words: pipe, file, bow, mouth, watch, force, shoe, lock, embassy.

We want a clue that relates to multiple team words but not to opponent/distractor/kill. Avoid cross association.

Potential clue "security": relates to lock, embassy (security), maybe watch (security watch), maybe force (security force). That's 4.

Check if "security" contains part of any word? No.

But could opponent have word "switch" (security switch?). Might be okay.

Distractor "server" but security server maybe but okay. Not prohibited; only must avoid being same or containing parts.

Kill words: train, calf, bank, bar, soul. Security not any.

Thus clue: security -> targets: lock, embassy, watch, force.

But watch relates to security watch. Force relates to security force. Embassy is a diplomatic security.

Is that solid? Could cause confusion with opponent words like "mass"? Not likely.

We must ensure clue is not one of opponent words (no). So provide.

Make sure format: CLUE: security

TARGETS: lock, embassy, watch, force

Yes.

jam, calf, train, shoe, server, pole, engine, bank, mouth, bar, switch, mass, spring, limousine, file, hood, duck, embassy, pipe, london, lock, watch, force, bow, soul
